mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates a robust labor market and healthy consumer demand, which directly supports corporate earnings and investor confidence, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of periods where PAYEMS shows positive month-over-month growth.

mechanism_narrative: A significant reduction in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) signals an aggressive monetary easing stance by the central bank, which lowers borrowing costs, stimulates economic activity, and boosts asset valuations, resulting in positive S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS drops by more than 0.5 percentage points over a 3-month period
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 40% of instances following a FEDFUNDS drop of 0.5 percentage points or more.

mechanism_narrative: Robust retail sales (RSAFS) combined with a low unemployment rate (UNRATE) signifies strong consumer health and purchasing power, which drives corporate revenues and profitability, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The interaction between positive RSAFS growth and UNRATE below 5% does not show a statistically significant positive correlation with SPX 20-day returns.

mechanism_narrative: Increasing industrial production (INDPRO) reflects expanding manufacturing and mining activity, indicating strong business investment and demand for goods, which translates into higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are not significantly positive when INDPRO is growing above its 6-month moving average.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a deteriorating labor market and potential economic recession, leading to reduced consumer spending, lower corporate profits, and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over a 3-month period
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 30% of instances where UNRATE rises by 0.5 percentage points or more over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent high inflation (CPIAUCSL) combined with rising interest rates (FEDFUNDS) erodes corporate profit margins and increases the cost of capital, leading to lower equity valuations and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth rate exceeds 3.0% AND FEDFUNDS has increased over the last 3 months
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive on average when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 3.0% and FEDFUNDS is also rising.

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing starts (HOUST) indicate robust demand in the housing sector, reflecting consumer confidence and investment, which stimulates related industries and contributes to overall economic growth, leading to positive S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month average is above 1.2 million units (seasonally adjusted annual rate)
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of periods where HOUST is above 1.2 million units.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in real gross domestic product (GDPC1) signifies a healthy and expanding economy, which underpins corporate revenue growth and profitability, thereby supporting higher equity valuations and positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are not significantly positive when GDPC1 growth is above its 10-year average.
